attacking_style: Pure flat-land sprinter who launches in final 200m, preferring to find gaps through the center rather than relying solely on leadout positioning. Does not attack proactively; waits for sprint finale setup.
team_tactics: Relies on high-powered leadouts (notably Jumbo-Visma's Van Aert pulling 65kph for 2km). Team controls pace into finales but rider shows positioning errors in chaotic sprints and crosswinds, requiring clean setup rather than improvisation.
terrain_preference: Exclusively pure flat finales. Even minor uncategorized rises or lumpy terrain expose serious climbing limitations. Crosswind situations reveal positioning vulnerabilities and inability to fight back when distanced.
vulnerability: Dropped on any categorized climb with flat remaining (14km+ observed). Poor recovery capacity on undulating stages. Positioning errors in echelons/crosswinds. Inferior final 200m kick versus peak sprinters like Ewan/Philipsen.
key_patterns: Repeatedly distanced on minor climbs throughout stages, marginal for non-pancake-flat finishes (5 instances)
Positioning errors in messy sprints: barriers, losing wheels, dropped in crosswinds (4 instances)
Loses head-to-head versus teammates and peak sprinters despite equal positioning (3 instances)
Finds success through center gaps when traditional leadout trains boxed in (2022 Tour win)
Team sprint hierarchy unclear; allowed to sprint independently alongside Van Aert (2019 Tour)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 distanced in crosswinds, 2022 showed form deficit on climbs and lost positioning battles. Recent performances suggest decline from 2019 peak competitive level.
